meta.title: 3 Days Bali Super Saver | Budget Bali Holiday Package from RM398 | Dhesu Travel
meta.description: Explore Bali on a budget with our 3 Days Super Saver package from RM398 per person. Visit Ubud rice terraces, art villages, coffee plantations and temples. Inclusive of hotel, meals, transfers and guide. Valid until March 2027.
meta.keywords: Bali budget package, 3 days Bali, cheap Bali holiday, Bali super saver, Ubud rice terrace tour, Bali trip Malaysia, Bali package RM398, Tegalalang rice terrace, Bali short trip

hero.title: 3 Days Bali
hero.intro: Discover the cultural heart of Bali without breaking the bank — rice terraces, sacred temples, art villages and a sunset seafood feast, all from just RM398 per person.
hero.heroImageAlt: Iconic Tegalalang rice terraces and tropical greenery in Ubud, Bali, Indonesia

excludes: ETIQA Travel Insurance Area 2 Gold RM41

overview.heading: The Perfect Budget Bali Escape for Malaysian Travellers
body: Bali has long been a favourite destination for Malaysians seeking a short tropical escape, and this 3 Days Super Saver package is designed to give you the very best of the island without stretching your budget. In just three days, you will journey through Ubud's highland villages, walk the famous Tegalalang Rice Terrace terraces, sip freshly brewed Luwak coffee at a local plantation, and explore intricate gold and silver jewellery at Celuk Art Village.

Every detail is taken care of — your airport greeting, comfortable hotel accommodation, daily breakfasts, all entrance fees and the services of a knowledgeable English-speaking driver-guide. Whether you are a first-time Bali visitor or returning for a quick cultural fix, this package delivers an authentic Balinese experience at unbeatable value. Easily extendable to 4 or more days, it is the ideal foundation for a longer Bali adventure.

items.title: Tegalalang Rice Terrace
items.body: Stroll through one of Asia's most photographed landscapes — the dramatic stepped terraces of Tegalalang, carved into the hillside north of Ubud. Breathe in the cool highland air and, if you are feeling adventurous, opt for the iconic Bali Swing for unforgettable aerial views over the rice fields.
items.imageAlt: Tegalalang stepped rice terraces with lush green paddies in Ubud, Bali

items.title: Celuk Gold & Silver Art Village
items.body: Celuk village is Bali's jewellery capital, home to generations of master goldsmiths and silversmiths. Watch artisans craft intricate rings, necklaces and earrings by hand, and pick up a unique Balinese keepsake at workshop prices you won't find elsewhere.
items.imageAlt: Balinese gold and silver handicraft artisan workshop in Celuk village

items.title: Luwak Coffee Plantation
items.body: Visit a traditional Balinese coffee estate and discover the story behind the world-famous Kopi Luwak. Sample a variety of locally grown coffees and herbal teas in a relaxed garden setting — a truly aromatic highlight of any Bali itinerary.
items.imageAlt: Luwak coffee and herbal tea tasting at a Balinese coffee plantation in Ubud

items.title: Ubud Royal Palace & Art Market
items.body: Explore the magnificent Ubud Royal Palace (Puri Saren Agung) with its intricate stone carvings and traditional Balinese courtyards, then browse the colourful Ubud Art Market next door for silk scarves, wooden carvings, handwoven baskets and local handicrafts.
items.imageAlt: Ornate gate and courtyards of the Ubud Royal Palace in Bali, Indonesia

items.title: Optional Uluwatu Sunset & Jimbaran Seafood
items.body: Enhance your first evening with an optional tour to the clifftop Uluwatu Temple, perched 70 metres above crashing ocean waves, followed by a legendary grilled BBQ seafood dinner on the sands of Jimbaran Bay as the sun dips below the Indian Ocean horizon.
items.imageAlt: Sunset view from Uluwatu cliff temple over the Indian Ocean, Bali

days.title: Arrive Bali — Airport Transfer & Leisure
days.description: Upon touching down at Ngurah Rai International Airport, your English-speaking driver-guide will greet you at the arrivals hall and transfer you directly to your hotel. After checking in, the rest of the afternoon and evening is yours to explore at your own pace — take a stroll along the beach, explore the buzzing Kuta or Legian streets, or simply relax by the hotel pool. For those looking to add excitement on arrival evening, an optional sunset tour is available at RM100 per person, covering a dramatic visit to the clifftop Uluwatu Temple and a memorable grilled BBQ seafood dinner on the famous Jimbaran Bay beachfront.
suggestedActivities: Relax at the hotel pool or beach
Explore Kuta or Legian streets independently
Optional: Grilled BBQ seafood dinner at Jimbaran Bay

days.title: Full Day Ubud Art Village & Rice Terrace Tour
days.description: After a hearty breakfast at the hotel, your private guide whisks you up into the cool Ubud highlands for a full day of cultural discovery. First stop is Celuk Village, where multi-generational goldsmiths and silversmiths craft exquisite jewellery by hand — the perfect place to find authentic Balinese accessories at workshop prices. Continue to a traditional coffee plantation to learn about Luwak coffee production and enjoy complimentary tastings of locally grown coffees and herbal teas. Next is the breathtaking Tegalalang Rice Terrace, one of the most scenic rice paddies in all of Asia, where a Bali Swing photoshoot is available at your own cost for those social-media-worthy shots. The afternoon takes you to the Ubud Royal Palace (Puri Saren Agung) to admire its elaborate stone carvings, followed by a visit to Pura Taman Saraswati, Bali's famous lotus temple dedicated to the goddess of art and wisdom. Round off the day at the lively Ubud Art Market, picking up silk scarves, rattan bags, wooden statues and other local handicrafts before heading back to the hotel.
suggestedActivities: Celuk Gold & Silver Art Village workshop tour
Pura Taman Saraswati lotus water temple

days.title: Souvenir Shopping & Departure
days.description: Enjoy your final Balinese breakfast at leisure before checking out of the hotel. The morning is free for any last-minute wandering — perhaps a beach walk, a spa treatment, or a smoothie bowl at one of Bali's famous cafes. Your guide will then take you on a quick stop at a quality souvenir shop where you can pick up last-minute gifts, spice blends, batik fabric and Balinese handicrafts before your driver transfers you to Ngurah Rai International Airport in comfortable time for your departure flight.
suggestedActivities: Morning leisure — beach, spa or café
Souvenir shop visit for gifts and local products

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Hotel Category
hotelOptions.subheading: All hotels are on a twin-sharing basis. Upgrade options available upon request.

tiers.label: 4-Star Comfort

tiers.label: 5-Star Premium
tiers.hotel: The Magani Hotel & Spa, Grand Istana Rama (4-star beachfront) or similar

tiers.label: 4-Star Private Pool Villa
tiers.hotel: Manca Villa Canggu, DiKubu Bali Villa or similar

whyBook.heading: Why Book Your Bali Trip Through Dhesu
whyBook.body: Dhesu Travel has been crafting unforgettable journeys for Malaysian travellers since 1988. Our Bali packages are fully private, meaning your group travels at your own pace with a dedicated driver-guide — no rushing with strangers on a tour bus.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Dhesu is a fully licensed Malaysian travel agency registered with MATTA and IATA, giving you the peace of mind that your holiday is in safe, professional hands.

points.title: Trusted Since 1988
points.body: With over 35 years of experience organising Bali holidays for Malaysian travellers, we know exactly what makes an unforgettable trip — and what to avoid.

points.title: 100% Private Tours
points.body: Your vehicle, your schedule. Unlike group coaches, every Dhesu Bali package is fully private so you can linger longer at the places you love and skip the ones you don't.

points.title: Best Value Guaranteed
points.body: Our Super Saver packages are priced to make Bali accessible to every Malaysian traveller, with transparent pricing and no hidden charges beyond the listed exclusions.

sections.heading: Why Bali is the Top Short-Haul Destination for Malaysians
sections.body: Just a 2.5-hour flight from Kuala Lumpur, Bali offers an extraordinary combination of lush tropical scenery, ancient Hindu temples, vibrant arts culture and world-class hospitality at a fraction of the cost of European or long-haul destinations. For Malaysian travellers seeking a meaningful short break, Bali's unique blend of spiritual atmosphere, warm local hospitality and incredible value makes it consistently one of the most-searched holiday destinations year after year.

sections.heading: What to Expect on a 3-Day Bali Itinerary
sections.body: Three days in Bali is enough to experience the island's cultural heartland in Ubud, including its iconic rice terraces, royal palace and traditional craft villages. You'll have time for a sacred temple visit, a coffee plantation tour and an evening beach seafood dinner. This Super Saver itinerary is ideal for first-timers wanting a taste of Bali, couples on a weekend escape, or anyone seeking a rejuvenating short break from the daily grind.

sections.heading: Extending Your Bali Super Saver Package
sections.body: This 3-day itinerary is designed to be easily extended — add a day for the Nusa Penida island trip, the spectacular Gates of Heaven at Lempuyang Temple, or the Bedugul Highlands and Tanah Lot sunset experience. Simply let our team know your preferred add-ons and we will customise the perfect Bali holiday to suit your schedule and budget.

items.question: Is this a private or group tour?
items.answer: This is a 100% private tour. You will have your own dedicated vehicle and English-speaking driver-guide for the entire trip — no joining strangers on a coach.

items.question: Are flights included in the RM398 price?
items.answer: No, flights are not included. The package covers hotel accommodation, meals as specified, all entrance fees, airport transfers and the services of a driver-guide. We recommend booking flights via AirAsia or Malindo Air for the best fares from Kuala Lumpur to Bali (Nguyen Rai International Airport, DPS).

items.question: What is the Bali Levy Tax?
items.answer: The Bali provincial government introduced a tourist levy of IDR 150,000 (approximately RM44) per international visitor, payable upon arrival. This is not included in the package price and must be paid separately.

items.question: Can I extend this 3-day package to more days?
items.answer: Absolutely. This package is designed to be easily extended. Popular add-ons include a Nusa Penida island day trip, the Gates of Heaven at Lempuyang Temple, or the Bedugul Highlands and Tanah Lot tour. Contact our team to customise your ideal Bali itinerary.

items.question: What is the best time of year to visit Bali?
items.answer: Bali's dry season runs from April to October, with July and August being the busiest months (note: high season surcharges apply). The shoulder months of April–June and September–October offer excellent weather with fewer crowds and better pricing.

items.question: Is travel insurance compulsory?
items.answer: Travel insurance is strongly recommended for all international travel. Our preferred partner ETIQA Travel Insurance Area 2 Gold is available for RM41 per person and can be added to your booking.
